sound. What it seems like to me the best
combination is to just describe the
sound simply at first. Let's do an
example. We'll say 808 bass. Then
describe the tamber or the character of
the sound. Like an 808 bass could be
short and punchy like a spins 808 or it
could be heavily distorted. That's
common in like trap metal and stuff like
that. I am a fan of trap metal so I'm
going to say distorted. And then you
want to give it the length. Now, it
doesn't always adhere to the length, but
I have noticed that any time that I put
a longer length, it will make longer
samples because it tends to go for 2 to
8 seconds. So, if I tell it 8 seconds,
it will usually do at least 7 to 13
seconds. So, for an 808, that's a short
sample. So, maybe this is a bad example,
but we'll we'll just put we'll just put
3 seconds. And I like to put it in
brackets and all caps. I can't confirm
whether or not that's the best way. I
tried this without the brackets and I
tried it in the brackets but not all
caps. And for me, what seems to work the
best is having it in all caps inside of
brackets because when I didn't put the
brackets there, it seemed to confuse the
sound. So once again, describe the
sound, describe the tambber, and tell it
the amount of seconds. And I would say
only really do the seconds thing if you
want your sample to be longer because it
